content: Llama-1B combined config (C CPU ops + cross-layer fusion) achieves 50.2 tok/s, up from 42.2 baseline (+19%)
All 4 Llama-1B configs pass 15/15 kill test against PyTorch
C CPU ops alone improve Llama-1B from 42.2 to 46.2 tok/s (+9.5%), cutting CPU time from 2.69ms to 0.46ms (6x faster)
Cross-layer fusion alone improves Llama-1B from 42.2 to 45.5 tok/s (+7.8%), ANE time drops from 16.73ms to 15.44ms
C CPU ops and cross-layer fusion stack nearly additively for Llama-1B
Llama-1B combined config per-token budget: ANE 15.51ms (75.5%), lm_head 4.62ms (22.5%), CPU 0.40ms (1.9%)
CPU is noise at 0.4ms in the combined Llama-1B config
Llama-1B bottleneck is ANE dispatch time (15.5ms) and lm_head (4.6ms)
Llama-1B lm_head uses 8 chunks dispatching 128K vocab, consuming 22.5% of per-token time
Llama-1B lm_head 8 chunks at 32MB each sits exactly at the SRAM cliff boundary
Fusing lm_head to 4 chunks (64MB each) would exceed the SRAM cliff and probably be slower
Vocab pruning from 128K to 50K would shrink lm_head weights from 256MB to 100MB, fitting in 3-4 SRAM-safe chunks
Vocab pruning could reduce lm_head time from 4.62ms to 1.8-2.2ms
Llama-1B in embedding mode (skip lm_head) would run at 15.91ms per token = 62.9 tok/s
The combined Llama-1B config uses 25 dispatches per token with 16 cross-layer + 2 standalone ANE dispatches
